name: Saor
recipeInstructions:
This marinating sauce for cooked fish or meats comes from Marco Fiorini at Palo d'Asti in San Francisco. The recipe originated in the area around Venice on the Adriatic coast where Fiorini trained.
Let the raisins soak for an hour, or until the wine is absorbed.
Heat the olive oil in a pan over medium heat.
Add the onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are tender but not browned, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the pine nuts or almonds and raisins, then the balsamic vinegar. Stir to combine.
Simmer for an additional 3-4 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Remove from heat and let cool completely.
Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days to 1 week.
Serve over fried fish such as fresh anchovies or baby red mullet. Refrigerate the fish several days before serving.
Saor is also excellent spooned over grilled chicken breasts, refrigerated for 2-3 days before serving, and used as a cold condiment with poached fish, marinated chicken, or vegetables.
PER TABLESPOON: 70 calories, 1 g protein, 3 g carbohydrate, 5 g fat (1 g saturated), 0 mg cholesterol, 1 mg sodium, 0 mg potassium.
